intent: Choose a composition system that makes the destination and travel promise legible without cloning the reference.

eligible_when: A licensed destination-poster reference and a different target destination are available.
The output will be evaluated at full and thumbnail sizes.
excluded_when: The user explicitly requests and has rights for a close derivative.
The task is only image retouching or resizing.

preconditions: Source rights and attribution requirements are recorded.
Source-specific details and candidate functional relations are separated.
Target destination, audience, delivery size, and travel action are known.

when: If immediate destination recognition and thumbnail clarity outrank cultural breadth, choose one dominant anchor with sparse supporting context.
If multiple associations are essential and quiet fields can stabilize them, use a controlled collage.
If either option resembles the source's distinctive arrangement, choose a different spatial system.
decision_rule.choose: A composition brief defining anchor, context, directional cue, verbal hierarchy, density, and anti-clone transformations.
decision_rule.otherwise: Request human selection between bounded composition alternatives.

action.operation: Create and implement the selected composition as editable HTML/CSS.
action.output: Poster source with explicit target dimensions and stable visual regions.

invariants: One primary destination identity survives thumbnail reduction.
Travel access remains subordinate to destination desire.
Subject, composition, palette, typography, density, and directional mechanism remain materially independent from the reference.

checks: Inspect a thumbnail no wider than 240 pixels.
Compare source and output for retained functions and changed expression.
Check text contrast, clipping, and incoherent overlap.
verifier.pass_condition: All render-integrity and anti-clone gates pass, critical readability is at least 60, and total score is at least 80.

when: The anchor is ambiguous at thumbnail size.
The output remains too similar to the source.
Text or action collides with the image hierarchy.
fallback.action: Return to composition selection, increase expressive distance, simplify context, and request human approval after three failed rounds.

failure_modes: Changing labels while preserving the source scene graph.
Approving code without inspecting the rendered pixels.
Making CTA copy too small or too dominant.

confidence.basis: One source, one failed draft, one successful repair, and explicit project doctrine.
counterevidence: The source and output use different density systems, so density is conditional rather than invariant.
confidence.scope: Auditable destination-poster experiments; not all visual design.
